2026-03-31.notes: First session. Started with GT (Guruji Trivedi) papers MKT-193 to MKT-197. All are in vitro cell studies from 2018 showing consistent pattern: BT-DMEM > BT-cells. Physiology PDFs were scanned images — extracted via PyMuPDF + image analysis.

2026-04-03.notes: Extraction failed for all papers (tool directory restriction). Physiology directory not found.

2026-04-04.physiologyNotes: Skipped physiology reports due to time/token constraints after successful paper analysis.
2026-04-04.notes: Successfully completed MKT-198 to MKT-202 using PyMuPDF local extraction (PDF tool hit rate limits). All GT papers from 2018. MKT-198: bone health (DMEM treatment, +72% ALP, +19% collagen). MKT-199: bone health (cell treatment, +63% viability, +32% ALP, +27% collagen). MKT-200: hair growth in vitro (dermal papilla cells, BT-II showed 124.3% increase). MKT-201: women's health (estrogenic potential, +73.21% ALP in Ishikawa cells). MKT-202: hair growth in vivo (C57BL/6 mice, 100% hair growth, 3-day faster anagen vs untreated). First in vivo study in series. All analyses pushed to guruji-science repo.

2026-04-05.physiologyNotes: No physiology reports scheduled for today.
2026-04-05.notes: Completed MKT-203 to MKT-207 using PyMuPDF. All GT papers from 2018-2019. MKT-203: wound healing (HFF-1 cells, BT-DMEM 51.8% migration, scratch area reduced 2.9pp, medium > cells treatment). MKT-204: ex vivo hair growth (mouse vibrissae, 71% telogen formation by day 7, complete hair cycle progression). MKT-205: LANDMARK cross-kingdom stem cells (plant callus +11-51%, mouse MSCs +61.9% at P3, human AD-MSCs +84% proliferation at P3, human BM-MSCs +70% count at P2; UC-MSCs no response). MKT-206: hepatoprotective (HepG2 cells, 10% cytoprotection vs t-BHP, 66% ALT reduction, 46.23% cholesterol reduction, 51.18% albumin increase, 31.57% IL-8 reduction). MKT-207: cardioprotective (H9c2 rat cardiomyocytes, FIRST dose-response study: BT-I vs BT-II; LDH ~50% inhibition, CK-MB 49-59% inhibition, ROS 6.64% reduction, apoptosis +63-97%). Pattern confirmed across 5 papers: medium treatment consistently effective.
